result:
**Goal: Add an Ising Model magnetic spin simulation mode (Shift+I toggle)**

The project has 10+ simulation modes covering cellular automata, particle systems, fluid dynamics, and agent-based models — but nothing from statistical mechanics. An Ising Model mode would introduce **phase transitions and thermodynamics**, a fundamentally different class of emergent behavior. Users would adjust temperature in real-time and watch the system snap between ordered ferromagnetic alignment and chaotic paramagnetic disorder at the critical temperature — one of the most visually dramatic phenomena in computational physics. It would use the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm, display live magnetization/energy stats, and render spin-up vs spin-down with colored block characters. This fills a clear conceptual gap in the simulation repertoire and pairs naturally with the existing architecture.
